content.problemStatement:
A pair of arrays AAA and BBB, both of length NNN, are called perfect if it is possible to form an array CCC of length NNN satisfying the following conditions:


Ci=AiC_i = A_iCi‚Äč=Ai‚Äč or Ci=BiC_i = B_iCi‚Äč=Bi‚Äč for each 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N
Ci<Ci+1C_i < C_{i + 1}Ci‚Äč<Ci+1‚Äč for each 1‚Č§i<N1 \le i < N1‚Č§i<N
You are given 222 arrays AAA and BBB of length NNN. It is guaranteed that each element from 111 to 2‚čÖN2 \cdot N2‚čÖN either occurs once in AAA or occurs once in BBB.

Find how many pairs LLL and RRR exist such that:


1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N
The pair of arrays A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] and B[L,R]B[L, R]B[L,R] are perfect.
Here, A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] represents the subrange [AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R][AL‚Äč,AL+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äč] of the array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of input contains a single integer NNN - the size of the arrays.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of LLL and RRR, such that corresponding subranges in AAA and BBB are perfect.
constraints: Ai‚Č†Aj,Bi‚Č†BjA_i \ne A_j, B_i \ne B_jAi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Aj‚Äč,Bi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Bj‚Äč for any 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N
Ai‚Č†BjA_i \ne B_jAi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Bj‚Äč for any 1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N1 \le i, j \le N1‚Č§i,j‚Č§N
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : All the pairs of LLL and RRR (satisfying 1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚Č§L‚Č§R‚Č§N) are perfect except L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3. For example, for L=1,R=2L = 1, R = 2L=1,R=2, we can choose C=[3,6]C = [3, 6]C=[3,6].
Test Case 2 : All the pairs LLL and RRR are perfect. For example, for L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3, we can choose C=[2,5,6]C = [2, 5, 6]C=[2,5,6].
